hypothesis: If a curated, standardized dataset (XDIP) of Fe K-edge X-ray absorption spectra paired with local atomic structure representations of iron-containing proteins is constructed using a semi-automated extraction and annotation pipeline, then machine learning models—including graph neural networks for structure-to-spectrum prediction and classical models for spectrum-to-property prediction—will demonstrate statistically meaningful baseline performance, because the dataset provides sufficient quantity and quality of paired spectral and structural information to capture the relationship between local iron coordination environments and their corresponding XAS signatures.
